mappings.rationale: Redfern's VSI provides a comprehensive overview of the Earth as a planet, directly covering orbital motions, rotation, the geoid, and geodetic measurements central to this section.
Lowrie covers the physical properties of the Earth including its shape, rotation, gravitational and tidal effects, and geodetic measurements—all core topics of this section.
Clifton's treatment of gravity is directly relevant to the Earth's gravitational field, Coriolis force, centrifugal effects, and tidal friction discussed in this section.
Rothery covers planetary science including orbital mechanics, planetary rotation, and the physical characteristics of Earth in the context of the solar system.

mappings.vsiAuthor: David George Bowers, Emyr Martyn Roberts
mappings.rationale: Directly relevant to tidal friction and the dynamic effects of Earth's rotation on tidal forces discussed in this section.
Covers planetary orbital dynamics and revolution around stars, directly relevant to the Earth's orbital motions and revolution about the Sun.
Bennett discusses geodetic measurement, the shape of the Earth, and satellite-based positioning systems that relate to the world geodetic system and measurement of geodetic parameters.
Lenton's overview of the Earth as an integrated system provides context for understanding the planet's orbital motions, physical shape, and geodetic properties.
